mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of GDPC1 growth remain below 10% of baseline volatility, or GDP growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.5% signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost consumer spending and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low UNRATE regimes show negative correlation (Pearson r < -0.2) or fail to exceed 15% of baseline volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal monetary policy tightening, which compresses equity multiples and causes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high CPI/FEDFUNDS regimes show positive correlation (Pearson r > 0.3) or outperform baseline returns by > 20%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input costs and boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high INDPRO regimes show negative correlation (Pearson r < -0.25) or fail to exceed 10% of baseline volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, reducing sectoral activity and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low HOUST regimes show positive correlation (Pearson r > 0.2) or outperform baseline returns by > 15%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market, which drives consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high PAYEMS/low UNRATE regimes show negative correlation (Pearson r < -0.3) or underperform baseline returns by > 25%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) below 430 billion dollars signals reduced household leverage, which lowers financial sector risk and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low RSAFS regimes show negative correlation (Pearson r < -0.2) or fail to exceed 10% of baseline volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 indicates strong supply-side momentum, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high INDPRO regimes show negative correlation (Pearson r < -0.25) or underperform baseline returns by > 20%.
